zh.lifeSummary: 當Rebecca Allen於大約1750年出生在美國田納西州布萊德時，她的父親Reuben Allen Jr30歲，母親Ingabo Hughes21歲。她於大約1777年與Francis Hughes在美國維吉尼亞州弗雷德里克結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和6個女兒的父母。她去世於1841年1月25日在美國田納西州格林享年92歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Rebecca Allen于大约1750年出生在美国田纳西州布萊德时，她的父亲Reuben Allen Jr30岁，母亲Ingabo Hughes21岁。她于大约1777年与Francis Hughes在美国弗吉尼亚州弗雷德里克结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和6个女儿的父母。她去世于1841年1月25日在美国田纳西州格林享年92岁。

nameMeanings.name: Allen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English, Old French personal name Alain, Alein (Old Breton Alan), from a Celtic personal name of great antiquity and obscurity. In England the personal name is now usually spelled Alan, the surname Allen; in Scotland the surname is more often Allan. From 1139 it was common in Scotland, where the surname also derives from Gaelic Ailéne, Ailín, from ail ‘rock’. The present-day frequency of the surname Allen in England and Ireland is partly accounted for by the popularity of the personal name among Breton followers of William the Conqueror, by whom it was imported first to Britain and then to Ireland. Saint Alan(us) was a 5th-century bishop of Quimper, who was a cult figure in medieval Brittany. Another Saint Al(l)an was a Cornish or Breton saint of the 6th century, to whom a church in Cornwall is dedicated.
English: occasionally perhaps from the rare Middle English femaje personal name Aline (Old French Adaline, Aaline), a pet form of ancient Germanic names in Adal-, especially Adalheidis (see Allis ).
French: variant of   Allain  , a cognate of 1 above, and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.

variants.name: Allenby
meanings: English: habitational name from Allonby (Cumberland). The placename is a post-Conquest compound of the French (Breton) personal name Alein (see Allen ) + Old Norse bȳ ‘village’, or the same word borrowed into Middle English.

variants.name: Allin
meanings: English (Devon): variant of   Allen  .
Swedish: variant of   Ahlin  .

variants.name: Alano
meanings: Italian (mainly Campania) and Hispanic (mainly Philippines): from the personal name Alano (see Allen ).

variants.name: Alan
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Allen  . This is the usual form of the personal name in England and Scotland.

variants.name: Allan
meanings: Scottish and northern English: variant of   Allen  . This is the more common form of the name in Scotland and northern England. See also   McAllen  .

History: An early bearer of this surname in North America was William Allan, a British soldier who took his family to the newly founded city of Halifax, NS, Canada, in 1749. His son John (1746–1805) was a Revolutionary soldier who eventually (1783) founded the settlement of Allan's Island, Passamaquoddy Bay, ME.

variants.name: Allain
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Jean-Paul, Adelard, Alban, Albenie, Alphonse, Andree, Cyrille, Donat, Emile, Euclide, Francois.

Breton and French: from the Old Breton personal name Alan (Old French Alain); see Allen . In France the personal name is spelled Alain, and this is also a less common variant of the surname (see Alain ).
English: variant of   Allen  , a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Alen
meanings: English: variant of   Allen  .
Galician (Alén): habitational name from one of the many places called Alén in Galicia (Spain), from Galician alén ‘beyond’.
Flemish and Dutch: patronymic from   Ale  .

variants.name: Allis
meanings: English: from the Middle English female personal name Alis, Alice (Old French Adeliz, Ahelis, Aalis, Aliz). Alice, together with its pet form Alison, was one of the most popular names in both England and Scotland from the 13th century until the end of the medieval period. The personal name is of ancient Germanic origin, brought to England from France by the Normans; it is a shortened form of ancient Germanic Adalheidis, which is composed of the elements adal ‘noble’ + haid ‘‘rank, worth’’.

variants.name: Alleyne
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Camille, Chantal, Julien, Marcell.

English: variant of   Allen  . This is an old-fashioned spelling, already established as a surname in England in Tudor times. Today this surname is found mainly in the West Indies, especially Barbados.

variants.name: Alling
meanings: English: either a variant of Allen , or a variant of Halling with loss of initial H-.
German: habitational name from either of two places called Alling, one in Bavaria and one in Austria.
Danish: habitational name from any of several places called Alling. The placename may be a derivative of al ‘alder’.

nameMeanings.name: Rebecca
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, from the Latin form of the Hebrew name Rebekah, borne by the wife of Isaac, who was the mother of Esau and Jacob (Genesis 24–27). The Hebrew root occurs in the Bible only in the vocabulary word marbek ‘cattle stall’, and its connection with the name is doubtful. In any case, Rebecca was Aramean, and the name probably has a source in Aramaic. It has always been common as a Jewish name; in England and elsewhere it began to be used also by Christians from the 14th century onwards and especially at the time of the Reformation, when Old Testament names became popular. It was very common among the Puritans in the 17th century, and has enjoyed a tremendous vogue in England since the latter part of the 20th century, among people of many different creeds. In Scotland this is found as an Anglicized form of Beathag .

historicEvents.description: Thomas Jefferson's American Declaration of Independence endorsed by Congress. Colonies declare independence.

historicEvents.title: Tennessee Becomes a State
historicEvents.description: On June 1, 1796, Tennessee became the 16th state.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Tennessee Secretart of State-From Territory to State

historicEvents.title: Movement to Washington D.C.
historicEvents.description: While the growth of the new nation was exponential, the United States didn’t have permanent location to house the Government. The First capital was temporary in New York City but by the second term of George Washington the Capital moved to Philadelphia for the following 10 years. Ultimately during the Presidency of John Adams, the Capital found a permanent home in the District of Columbia.
France sells Louisiana territories to U.S.A.

historicEvents.title: Making States Equal
historicEvents.description: The Missouri Compromise helped provide the entrance of Maine as a free state and Missouri as a slave state into the United States. As part of the compromise, slavery was prohibited north of the 36°30′ parallel, excluding Missouri.

historicEvents.title: The Hermitage is Built
historicEvents.description: The Hermitage located in Nashville, Tennessee was a plantation owned by President Andrew Jackson from 1804 until his death there in 1845. The Hermitage is now a museum.

en.lifeSummary: When Rebecca Allen was born about 1750, in Bledsoe, Tennessee, United States, her father, Reuben Allen Jr, was 30 and her mother, Ingabo Hughes, was 21. She married Francis Hughes about 1777, in Frederick, Virginia, United States. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 6 daughters. She died on 25 January 1841, in Greene, Tennessee, United States, at the age of 92.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Rebecca Allen est née environ 1750, à Bledsoe, Tennessee, États-Unis, son père, Reuben Allen Jr, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Ingabo Hughes, avait 21 ans. Elle a épousé Francis Hughes environ 1777, à Frederick, Virginie, États-Unis. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 6 filles. Elle est décédée le 25 janvier 1841, à Greene, Tennessee, États-Unis, à l’âge de 92 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Rebecca Allen um 1750 in Bledsoe, Tennessee, Vereinigte Staaten geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Reuben Allen Jr 30 und ihre Mutter Ingabo Hughes 21. Sie hat um 1777 in Frederick, Virginia, Vereinigte Staaten Francis Hughes geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 6 Töchter. Sie starb am 25. Januar 1841 im Alter von 92 Jahren in Greene, Tennessee, Vereinigte Staaten.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Rebecca Allen è nata il circa 1750 nel Bledsoe, Tennessee, Stati Uniti d'America, sua padre, Reuben Allen Jr, aveva 30 anni e sua madre, Ingabo Hughes, aveva 21 anni. Lei sposato Francis Hughes circa 1777, nel Frederick, Virginia, Stati Uniti d'America. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 6 figlie. Morì il 25 gennaio 1841, nel Greene, Tennessee, Stati Uniti d'America, all'età di 92 anni.

ko.Frederick, Virginia Colony: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 프레더릭크
ko.lifeSummary: Rebecca Allen(이)가 대략 1750년 미국 테네시 주 블레드소에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Reuben Allen Jr(은)는 30세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Ingabo Hughes(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 대략 1777년 미국 버지니아 주 프레더릭크에서 Francis Hughes(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1841년 1월 25일 미국 테네시 주 그린에서 92세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Rebecca Allen родилась около 1750 г. в местности бледсо, Теннесси, Соединенные штаты, её отцу по имени Reuben Allen Jr было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Ingabo Hughes, 21. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Francis Hughes около 1777 г. в местности фредирикк, Вирджиния, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 6 дочерей. Она умерла 25 января 1841 г. в местности грин, Теннесси, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 92 лет.
